Petition For Determination Of Incapacity Of Settlor; To Compel Production Of Estate Planning Documents And Trust Records; And To Compel Trustee'S Acco
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Continued To Jul-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Erica S. Cooper, via Zoom, for and with Michael Beallo, Petitioner; Amy Harrington and Albert John Nicora, via Zoom, for and with Katherine Beallo, Objector; Andrew Zabronsky, via Zoom, for Richard George, Respondent.

The court advises that it believes that venue is proper and notes the motions set in this matter for Jul-07-2022 and proposes a continuance of the instant petition to a date after the motions have been adjudicated. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the instant petition. The court queries Mr. Zabronsky regarding whether the Trustee is willing to provide an accounting for the time that he has served in any fiduciary capacity. Mr. Zabronsky offers testimony regarding the court's query and reports that the parties, with the exception of one party in the conservatorship matter, have agreed to mediation. The court confirms that the related matter in Contra Costa County has been dismissed and filed in Monterey County with a Jun-29-2022 hearing date.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Determination, etc., is continued to Jul-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib CSR#: 13714 T: 415.601.7054 E: apourtabib@gmail.com.

Petition For Determination Of Incapacity Of Settlor; To Compel Production Of Estate Planning Documents And Trust Records; And To Compel Trustee'S Acco
Off Calendar Apr-18-2022 Continued To May-09-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington, and Albert Nicora, via Zoom, for Katherine Beallo, via Zoom, Objector; Erica Cooper, via Zoom, for Michael Beallo, via Zoom, Petitioner; Andrew Zabronsky, via Zoom, for Richard George, Trustee; Wayne McIntosh, via Zoom, Jack Beallo, via Zoom, Beneficiary.

The court summarizes its position regarding whether venue is proper. Mr. Zabronsky reports that the parties believed that the court had determined that venue was proper at the previous calling of this matter. The court reminds Mr. Zabronsky that at the previous hearing, the court noted that venue could not be waived. Mr. Zabronsky and Ms. Harrington offer additional testimony as to why they believe that venue is proper. Ms. Cooper reports that she believes that this matter should be adjudicated in either Nevada or Contra Costa County. Mr. Zabronsky advises that he is willing to file additional papers in support of his position and asks for a continuance in order to do so.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Determination, etc., is continued to May-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Mr. Zabronsky is instructed to file his papers in support of his position by May-04-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

Petition For Determination Of Incapacity Of Settlor; To Compel Production Of Estate Planning Documents And Trust Records; And To Compel Trustee'S Acco
Off Calendar Feb-28-2022 Continued To Apr-18-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Incapacity Of Settlor; To Compel Production Of Estate Planning Documents And Trust Records; And To Compel Trustee'S Acco
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Continued To Feb-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington, Esq., counsel for Katherine Beallo, Settlor/Objector (appearing by video conference); Albert Nicora, Esq., counsel for Katherine Beallo, Settlor/Objector (appearing by video conference); Erica Cooper, Esq., counsel for and with Michael Beallo, Beneficiary/Petitioner (appearing separately by video conference); Andrew Zabronsky, Esq. counsel for Richard George, Trustee (appearing by video conference); Wayne McIntosh, Esq., counsel for and with Jack Beallo, beneficiary (appearing separately by video conference).

The Court questions venue at the outset to which Mr. Zabronsky responds, advising that his client, the Trustee, represents by declaration filed with the Court that his principal residence is San Francisco and that the principal place of administration of the Trust is San Francisco.

Ms. Cooper responds, noting various circumstances that indicate the Trustee resides in Truckee, California and that she has been unable to verify any presence of the Trustee in San Francisco.

At the Court's query, Mr. McIntosh advises that he represents Jake Beallo, trust beneficiary, and is appearing for the sole purposes of requesting special notice of all proceedings. The Court acknowledges the same.

In response to Ms. Cooper and the Court, Mr. Zabronsky advises the Trustee's residence in San Francisco is a high-rise apartment building as opposed to commercial office space. Ms. Harrington argues for venue in San Francisco and contends that Ms. Cooper's clients have waived venue by their general appearance in this matter.

The Court notes that venue cannot be waived and that in light of new evidence concerning the residence of the Trustee, the Court will continue the matter to February 28, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for further review.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Dillingham. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition For Determination Of Incapacity Of Settlor; To Compel Production Of Estate Planning Documents And Trust Records; And To Compel Trustee'S Acco
